mappings.rationaleAI: The Earth's core covering magnetic field generation through dynamo theory, core-mantle interactions, iron/nickel composition, and extreme conditions addresses the magnetic field (B), electrical currents from the core (C.3), heat sources (D.1), heat flow from interior (D.2), and mechanical properties (E).

Earth's magnetic field, polarity reversals, magnetic materials, and Gilbert's De Magnete directly address measurement and representation (B.1), sources and characteristics (B.2), and variations including reversals (B.3).

Seismology revealing Earth's internal structure through seismic wave analysis, earthquake mechanisms, and the Richter scale directly addresses mechanical properties (E), seismic waves (E.2), and deformation of materials (G).

Plate tectonics covering mantle convection, subduction, mountain building, and volcanic activity addresses geologic aspects of heat flow (D.3), response to long-duration stresses (E.6), and deformation of crustal materials (G).
Solar wind interaction with Earth's magnetosphere producing auroras and magnetic storms addresses variations in the main magnetic field including magnetic storms (B.3).
The history of heat science, thermodynamics, and heat transfer mechanisms address the Earth's thermal properties (D) and sources of heat (D.1).
